leads.name: Cardinal Rule Press (Maria Dismondy)
leads.notes: Cardinal Rule Press is a southeast Michigan children's book publisher founded in 2015 by former teacher and award-winning author Maria Dismondy. The press has grown to more than two dozen purpose-driven picture books focused on children's mental wellness and diverse representation, with over one million copies sold worldwide, Ingram distribution, and a national retail footprint. Michigan-based and regionally aligned with Cushing-Malloy, Cardinal Rule is a multi-title annual print account with print runs in the hundreds to thousands per title. Outreach angle: one dedicated manufacturing contact for a Michigan press that competes on consistency and quality -- same person from quote to delivery across every title in their season.
leads.pitch: Cardinal Rule Press is a southeast Michigan children's publisher with 2+ dozen titles, 1 million+ copies sold, and Ingram distribution -- a multi-title annual print account with Michigan geography and high repeat-print volume. Outreach angle: one dedicated manufacturing contact for a Michigan neighbor press that competes on quality and consistency.

leads.name: Honey Blossom Press (Keisha Mennefee)
leads.notes: Honey Blossom Press is a boutique independent imprint launched by literary strategist Keisha Mennefee to champion diverse storytellers, with a stated plan to release 20 titles in its first year -- a volume that immediately puts them in multi-title manufacturing territory. Mennefee has worked with established authors including Kennedy Ryan and Jada Pinkett Smith, giving the press a high-profile launch profile and credibility that drives realistic print runs. The press focuses on culturally resonant fiction and nonfiction. Outreach angle: a new press launching at scale needs a manufacturing partner with single-contact consistency from day one -- Cushing-Malloy can build that relationship before the first title ships.
leads.pitch: Honey Blossom Press is a new boutique imprint from literary strategist Keisha Mennefee targeting 20 titles in year one -- a launch-at-scale opportunity before they lock in a manufacturing partner. Outreach angle: get in early with a press building a multi-title annual cadence and offer the single-contact consistency that new publishers need most.

leads.name: Cornerstone Press (UW-Stevens Point)
leads.source: AAUP Teaching Press Directory / Education Channel
leads.notes: Cornerstone Press is the university press of the University of Wisconsin-Stevens Point, established in 1984 as the first teaching press in the UW system. Now publishing 30+ titles annually across poetry, short story, creative nonfiction, and prize collections, Cornerstone is one of the most active undergraduate student-staffed presses in the United States, with titles earning recognition from PEN America, the Midwest Book Awards, and national review outlets including the New York Times Book Review. Stevens Point, WI is approximately 5 hours from Ann Arbor -- Midwest proximity and a steady 30+ annual title volume make this a strong recurring manufacturing account. Outreach angle: a fellow Midwest literary institution with consistent multi-title volume that benefits from one manufacturing contact who knows their standards across every title.
leads.pitch: Cornerstone Press at UW-Stevens Point publishes 30+ titles annually and is the first teaching press in the UW system -- 5 hours from Ann Arbor with a consistent multi-title print volume and recognition from PEN America and Midwest Book Awards. Outreach angle: a peer Midwest institutional press with steady annual volume and geographic proximity.

leads.name: Sine Nomine Publishing (Kevin Crawford)
leads.notes: Sine Nomine Publishing is a one-person independent game and book publishing house founded in 2010 by Kevin Crawford, producing OSR (Old School Renaissance) tabletop RPG rulebooks and supplements across multiple successful Kickstarter campaigns. Crawford produces 3-5 titles annually in hardcover and softcover formats, with proven print runs and consistent print quality requirements. The press has logged six consecutive years of profitability and a perfect Kickstarter delivery record. RPG game books have specialized production requirements: specific trim sizes, heavy paper for rulebooks, durable binding for gaming use. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's offset hardcover capabilities and single-contact guidance on paper weight and binding durability for a publisher who prints to a technical standard his gaming audience expects.
leads.pitch: Sine Nomine Publishing is a profitable one-person RPG press with 6 consecutive years of profitability and 5+ successful Kickstarters, producing 3-5 hardcover titles annually with specific paper and binding requirements. Outreach angle: offset hardcover printing guidance for a publisher whose audience expects durable, premium physical rulebooks.

leads.name: Trouser Press Books
leads.notes: Trouser Press Books is a New York-based independent publisher of music journalism and fiction, with roots going back to the Trouser Press rock fanzine founded in 1974. Now producing active titles distributed through Ingram to bookstores and libraries, the press publishes in paperback and ebook with Editorial Director Ira Robbins at the helm. The publishing heritage and niche music journalism focus mean their books have a dedicated readership willing to pay for quality physical editions. CLMP listed with active Bookshop.org storefront. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's offset paperback quality with 20-day production for a press building a serious Ingram-distributed catalog in the music book niche.
leads.pitch: Trouser Press Books is a NY-based independent music journalism publisher with Ingram distribution and a heritage dating to 1974 -- active publishing program with dedicated niche readership. Outreach angle: offset paperback quality with 20-day production for an Ingram-distributed catalog in the music book specialty space.

leads.name: Ooligan Press (Portland State University)
leads.source: AAUP Teaching Press Directory / Education Channel
leads.notes: Ooligan Press is the graduate teaching press of Portland State University's Book Publishing program, producing 10-12 titles annually across literary fiction, nonfiction, and children's books with Ingram distribution and national critical recognition. As a student-staffed press producing real commercial titles -- not just academic exercises -- Ooligan has clear, recurring print manufacturing needs. The press actively evaluates manufacturing partners and has a dedicated acquisitions, production, and marketing team. Pacific Northwest based with national distribution. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's single-contact model is ideal for a student production team managing multiple simultaneous titles -- one contact eliminates the coordination complexity that multi-handoff printers create.
leads.pitch: Ooligan Press at Portland State produces 10-12 titles annually as a graduate teaching press with Ingram distribution -- a consistent multi-title print account with national distribution. Outreach angle: single-contact manufacturing is ideal for a student production team managing multiple simultaneous titles.

leads.name: Stone Pier Press
leads.notes: Stone Pier Press is a San Francisco-based independent publisher focused on food systems, sustainability, and environmental nonfiction -- a growing specialty niche with a loyal readership willing to pay premium prices for quality physical books. The press operates an active publishing program with a bookstore and distributes titles nationally. Their food and environmental focus means titles benefit from high-quality paper and printing that matches the premium positioning of sustainability-conscious products. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's offset quality for a specialty nonfiction press whose readers expect the physical product to match the book's premium values.
leads.pitch: Stone Pier Press is an SF-based food and environmental nonfiction publisher with a national distribution footprint and active publishing program -- specialty nonfiction readers expect premium physical editions. Outreach angle: offset quality for a press whose sustainability-focused readership expects the physical book to match the book's values.

leads.name: Schaffner Press
leads.notes: Schaffner Press is an independent Tucson, Arizona publisher founded in 2001 by former literary agent Timothy Schaffner, with nearly 100 titles of literary fiction, nonfiction, and poetry published over two decades. Publishing 6-8 titles annually across health, environment, social justice, biography, and literary fiction, the press has built a distinctive catalog of socially relevant works with national distribution. The press publishes both fiction and non-fiction of social concern. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's single-contact model is ideal for a small press with a two-person editorial team managing multiple simultaneous titles -- one manufacturing partner who knows their standards across a 6-8 title annual cadence.
leads.pitch: Schaffner Press is a Tucson independent press with 20+ years of operation, nearly 100 titles published, and 6-8 books annually across literary fiction and socially relevant nonfiction. Outreach angle: single-contact manufacturing for a seasoned small press operating at consistent annual volume.

leads.name: CLASH Books (Leza Cantoral / Christoph Paul)
leads.notes: CLASH Books is a Troy, New York-based independent literary press founded by authors Leza Cantoral and Christoph Paul, publishing literary fiction, horror, nonfiction, and poetry with a Spring/Summer 2026 active release schedule. The press celebrates new and diverse voices across all genres with a reputation for publishing books that take real creative risks. CLASH Books is CLMP listed with a national readership. Active 2026 titles in production signal current manufacturing demand. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's ability to handle literary fiction across multiple trim sizes and binding formats for a press that publishes across multiple genres simultaneously.
leads.pitch: CLASH Books is a Troy NY indie press publishing literary fiction, horror, and poetry with active Spring/Summer 2026 titles -- a risk-taking press with multi-genre simultaneous production. Outreach angle: flexible offset capabilities for a press that publishes across literary fiction, horror, and nonfiction simultaneously.

leads.name: Whisk(e)y Tit (Miette Gillette)
leads.notes: Whisk(e)y Tit is a Vermont-based one-woman literary press founded in 2014 by Miette Gillette, publishing 50+ books to date of experimental and eccentrically literary fiction, poetry, and nonfiction that mainstream publishers have rejected for being 'just a little too weird.' The press revives out-of-print books alongside first publications. Poets & Writers and CLMP listed with consistent annual output. As a one-person press, Gillette handles all production decisions herself, making direct outreach to the publisher essential. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's white-glove guidance is exactly what a solo publisher needs -- one contact who walks her through every production decision without requiring her to manage multiple relationships.
leads.pitch: Whisk(e)y Tit is a Vermont one-woman literary press with 50+ books published since 2014, producing experimental and literary titles that mainstream presses pass on -- Poets & Writers and CLMP listed. Outreach angle: white-glove single-contact guidance for a solo publisher who makes all production decisions herself.

leads.name: Gavin Kaysen (Soigné Hospitality Group)
leads.notes: Gavin Kaysen is a James Beard Award-winning chef and founder of the Soigné Hospitality Group in Minneapolis, Minnesota -- a Midwest culinary authority with a national following. Kaysen has self-published his cookbook 'At Home' through his own website (gavinkaysen.com), selling directly to his audience without a traditional publisher. A self-published chef with a proven audience, national name recognition, and a direct-to-consumer sales model has clear print manufacturing needs at each edition cycle. Minneapolis is approximately 6 hours from Ann Arbor -- Midwest proximity applies. Outreach angle: offset cookbook quality for a Midwest chef with proven self-publishing history who controls his own print relationship.
leads.pitch: Gavin Kaysen is a James Beard Award-winning Minneapolis chef who self-publishes his cookbook 'At Home' through his own website -- a proven direct-to-consumer model with Midwest proximity. Outreach angle: offset cookbook manufacturing for a Midwest culinary authority who owns his print relationship.

leads.name: Middle West Press
leads.notes: Middle West Press LLC is an independent micro-publisher based in Johnston, Iowa, producing 1-4 titles annually of non-fiction, fiction, journalism, and poetry with a focus on war literature and regional Midwestern storytelling. CLMP listed with an active Bookshop.org storefront and open submission calls for 2026 poetry and micro-prose anthologies. Johnston, Iowa is approximately 4 hours from Ann Arbor -- Midwest proximity applies. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's 250-copy offset minimum with white-glove guidance for a micro press where the publisher handles all production decisions.
leads.pitch: Middle West Press is a Johnston Iowa micro-publisher producing 1-4 titles annually, CLMP listed, with active 2026 open submission calls -- Midwest proximity and micro-press scale. Outreach angle: 250-copy offset minimum with white-glove guidance for a publisher who makes all production decisions independently.

leads.name: Soberscove Press (Julia Klein)
leads.notes: Soberscove Press is a Chicago-based independent publisher of art and culture books founded by Julia Klein in 2009, with distribution through D.A.P. (Distributed Art Publishers) -- a premium distribution channel indicating a serious trade presence. The press publishes books in collaboration with artists that explore documentation, process, and connections between historical and contemporary themes. Chicago is 3.5 hours from Ann Arbor -- strong Midwest proximity. CLMP listed with an active online store. Art and culture books often require specific paper selections and finishing that non-specialist printers struggle to execute. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's production guidance for art-focused books -- paper, binding, and finishing that matches D.A.P.-level distribution standards.
leads.pitch: Soberscove Press is a Chicago art/culture publisher distributed through D.A.P. -- premium distribution indicates serious trade presence, and art books require specialized paper and finishing. 3.5 hours from Ann Arbor. Outreach angle: production guidance for art-focused books that match D.A.P.-level quality standards.

leads.name: Rescue Press
leads.notes: Rescue Press is an independent editor-run literary press founded in 2009 in Milwaukee, now based across Iowa City, Chicago, and Cleveland, publishing experimental and investigative writing with national distribution through Itasca Books. The press published 'Lonesome Ballroom' by Madeline McDonnell in Spring 2026, featured in Electric Literature's 'Must-Read Small Press Books of Spring 2026.' Editors Daniel Khalastchi and Caryl Pagel are the publisher contacts. CLMP listed with consistent annual literary output and national critical attention. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's offset literary quality with 20-day production for a Midwestern-anchored literary press with active 2026 titles.
leads.pitch: Rescue Press is an Iowa City/Chicago/Cleveland literary press with a Spring 2026 title featured in Electric Literature -- editor-run, CLMP listed, Itasca Books distributed, with Midwestern roots. Outreach angle: 20-day offset production for a Midwestern-anchored literary press with active 2026 publishing.

leads.name: Unencumbered Press (Stephanie Johnson Lawson)
leads.notes: Unencumbered Press is an independent publishing house founded in 2022 by Stephanie Johnson Lawson, focused on books that explore unconventional perspectives on living and personal freedom. The press is actively publishing and growing, with a dedicated publisher who manages editorial and production decisions. As a recently launched press under 5 years old, they are actively building vendor relationships and evaluating manufacturing partners. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's white-glove guidance is ideal for a new independent publisher building her production process from scratch -- one contact walks her through every manufacturing decision.
leads.pitch: Unencumbered Press is a newly founded independent publisher (2022) actively building vendor relationships -- a recently established press under 5 years old is a prime target for manufacturing partner outreach. Outreach angle: white-glove guidance for a publisher building her production process from scratch.

leads.name: Rebel Goose Books (Susan Pi)
leads.notes: Rebel Goose Books is an independent children's publisher founded by Susan Pi (Master of Publishing, Simon Fraser University), recently announced as a new IBPA member and landing a US distributor ahead of its first book launch -- clear signals of an active publisher entering the US market. Based in Nanaimo, BC, Canada, the press publishes books inspiring young minds to dream boldly and question freely. The US distribution relationship signals immediate US print manufacturing relevance: a Canadian press with US wholesale distribution needs US-based printing to eliminate cross-border freight on every fulfillment cycle. Outreach angle: US-based printing at Cushing-Malloy eliminates cross-border freight for a Canadian publisher now active in US wholesale distribution.
leads.pitch: Rebel Goose Books is a newly launched Canadian children's publisher that just landed a US distributor -- a clear signal of US market entry that makes US-based printing directly relevant. Outreach angle: eliminate cross-border freight by printing in the US for a Canadian press with active US wholesale distribution.

leads.name: Calypso Editions
leads.notes: Calypso Editions is an independent Houston, Texas publisher of poetry, fiction, and books in translation with active open reading periods and a Bookshop.org storefront. The press publishes beautiful print books alongside developing ebook editions, with a stated interest in emerging and established writers at every career stage. Active open reading periods signal consistent title acquisition for 2026 production. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's short-run offset quality for a poetry and literary fiction press investing in premium physical editions.
leads.pitch: Calypso Editions is a Houston poetry/fiction/translation press with active 2026 open reading periods -- consistent title acquisition signals near-term print manufacturing demand. Outreach angle: short-run offset quality for a press investing in premium physical editions alongside digital.

leads.name: Noemi Press
leads.notes: Noemi Press is an independent nonprofit literary publisher founded in 2002, based across Tucson, Arizona and Blacksburg, Virginia, producing approximately 5 titles annually across poetry, fiction, and essays with a focus on writers from underrepresented communities. The press administers the Noemi Press Awards in Poetry and the Etel Adnan Poetry Prize. CLMP listed with national critical recognition from PEN America and other literary organizations. The steady 5-title annual cadence represents consistent manufacturing demand. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's short-run offset quality for a nonprofit literary press with consistent annual production and award-recognized titles.
leads.pitch: Noemi Press is an established nonprofit literary publisher (founded 2002) with 5 annual titles across poetry, fiction, and essays -- CLMP listed with PEN America recognition and active prize programs. Outreach angle: reliable short-run offset manufacturing for a nonprofit press with consistent annual production.

leads.name: Messy Press (Erica Harrison)
leads.notes: Messy Press is a children's book publishing studio founded in 2025 by Erica Harrison and Adam Thompson, producing vibrant illustrated non-fiction, gift, and activity books for young readers. Harrison has 20+ years of children's book design and illustration experience and has partnered with Walker Books for distribution -- Walker is a major international children's book publisher, signaling serious trade intent. IBPA member. Based in New Zealand but with Walker Books US and UK distribution indicating North American market entry. Outreach angle: US-based printing at Cushing-Malloy eliminates Pacific shipping costs for North American distribution; Walker Books partnership signals real print volumes for the US market.
leads.pitch: Messy Press is a newly founded NZ children's press (2025) that just partnered with Walker Books for distribution -- Walker's US distribution footprint means US-based printing is directly relevant for North American fulfillment. Outreach angle: eliminate Pacific freight by printing US inventory at Cushing-Malloy.

leads.name: R.A. Dines (Rachel Dines)
leads.notes: R.A. Dines is the pen name of Rachel Dines, a former tech executive and Forrester Research analyst turned full-time author who self-published her debut middle-grade science fiction novel 'How I Hacked the Moon,' a BookLife Prize semi-finalist. Dines writes on Substack about her publishing journey and has a growing author platform as a former tech exec with credibility in both the tech and creative writing communities. As a self-published debut author building her author brand, she is actively managing her own print relationship and evaluating options as her readership grows. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's white-glove first-time author guidance for a tech-savvy self-publisher ready to graduate from POD to a quality offset run.
leads.pitch: R.A. Dines is a former tech exec who self-published debut middle-grade sci-fi 'How I Hacked the Moon' (BookLife Prize semi-finalist) and documents her publishing journey publicly on Substack -- a tech-savvy self-publisher building her platform. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's first-author guidance for a self-publisher ready to explore offset quality beyond POD.
